channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: INXS to Receive Australia’s Ted Albert Award
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: "Welcome to our podcast, where we dive into the latest music news. Today, we're celebrating a legendary Australian band that's left an indelible mark on the music industry. INXS is this year's recipient of the prestigious Ted Albert Award for Outstanding Services to Australian Music.
[Soft music transitions]
Host: "With over 75 million albums sold worldwide, INXS is one of the most successful bands to emerge from Australia. Formed in 1977, the sextet consisted of Andrew, Jon, Tim Farriss, Garry Beers, Kirk Pengilly, and the late Michael Hutchence. They climbed to the top of the rock world, with five top 20 albums on the Billboard 200 and a number one hit on the Billboard Hot 100 with 'Need You Tonight'.
[Music clip: "Need You Tonight" plays briefly]
Host: "INXS also scored big in the UK, with six top 10 albums, including a number one with 'Welcome To Wherever You Are'. They even took home a BRIT Award in 1991 for best international group. According to Jenny Morris, chair of APRA, 'INXS are truly one-of-a-kind', and their legacy lives on, inspiring new generations and bringing that unmistakable Aussie spirit to fans around the world.
[Music clip: "Never Tear Us Apart" plays briefly]
Host: "The band may have called it a day in 2012, but their music lives on through syncs, multi-media projects, and the dedication of their superfans. The Ted Albert Award is one of the Australian music industry's highest honors, and previous recipients include Paul Kelly, The Seekers, and Kylie Minogue.
[Outro music plays]
Host: "INXS will receive the Ted Albert Award at the 2026 APRA Music Awards on April 29th at Sydney's Hordern Pavilion. Congratulations to this iconic band on their well-deserved recognition. Thanks for tuning in, and we'll catch you on the next episode."
